title: ESP32-C3 drives a relay via NPN
prompt: I need my ESP32-C3 to switch a 5 V relay coil on and off. The GPIO can't drive a coil directly, so use an NPN transistor as a low-side switch: GPIO to the base through a base resistor, the relay coil between 5 V and the collector, and a freewheeling diode across the coil to catch the turn-off spike. Firmware energizes the relay when active. For the bench check drive the base on.
